challenge_text: Run 'shellmux pub <dir> control --delay 5 reboot' and verify it arrives ~5s later while top/ps shows ~0% scheduler CPU for the whole interval. SUCCESS: message arrives within documented resolution of the deadline; CPU ~0% during the wait.

friction_points.description: pstree is not installed in the shellmux-dev container. Not needed for this persona (top/ps/ls/cat all present and sufficient), but an operator who reaches for 'pstree -p $BPID' to eyeball the scheduler tree during the wait gets 'command not found'. Pure ergonomics; the documented introspection (ls/cat/top/ps) all work.
friction_points.workaround: Use 'ps --ppid' or 'top -bn1'; both present and show the 0.0% scheduler rows.

wished_for: A one-line 'next deadline in Ns' readout (currently you compute it yourself from the run_at_ms in the deferred filename minus now); the raw filename is honest but an operator does ms arithmetic in their head.
DEMO Beat-2 hardcodes a sleep before sub is ready (the known-punted F5); a 'sub is live' marker would let a script publish deterministically instead of sleeping.
what_worked_well: The exact persona/DEMO Beat-2 incantation worked verbatim: serve + sub + 'echo reboot-now | pub control --delay 5' -> pub rc=0, deferred file staged immediately, 'reboot-now' delivered.

Delivery landed 21ms past the scheduled deadline (tight poll measurement; run_at_ms - pub_ms = 5010ms, fire - run_at = 21ms). Independently corroborated by tests/deferred_pub.sh D1/D2 (~35-38ms). Well inside the documented '~40ms wake-driven, lands on the second'.

Idle CPU was 0% the entire 5s wait by THREE measurements: ps %cpu sum over the 6-proc broker tree = 0 at every 1s sample; top showed the socat/scheduler rows at 0.0% and %Cpu 100.0 id; tests/deferred_pub.sh D3 = 0 CPU ticks over 2s. No busy-spin, no timer wheel.
deferred/ self-documents: file named <run_at_ms>.<seq>, visible via ls during the wait, cleared (mv'd out) after firing exactly once.

A late subscriber that connected AFTER the deferred pub but BEFORE the deadline still received the message - confirms the deadline is broker-side and decoupled from subscriber connection timing, exactly an operator's mental model.

Full N=5000 chaos gate re-run green: missed=0 dup=0 ontime=5000; all 3 must-fail controls (naivesleep 120/120 miss, drainfirst 120/120 miss, nocommit dup=1770) failed on their predicted axis; idle CPU 0.00%.
comparison_to_expectations: Met expectations and matched round-001's 9/10. The headline behavior (delay lands on the second at 0% CPU) is rock-solid and now backed by three independent CPU measurements rather than one. Every round-001 fix I could reach stayed fixed. I did not raise to 10 because the only residual friction (manual ms arithmetic on the deferred filename to know 'how long left', and the still-punted readiness-signal sleep in the demo) is unchanged from round-001 - nothing regressed, but nothing new delighted me on the timing path either.
free_text: Black-box evidence summary. Challenge SUCCESS: 'pub control --delay 5 reboot' staged deferred/1782185515755.<seq> (run_at = pub+5010ms), delivered 'reboot' 21ms past deadline, broker tree at 0% CPU across all five 1s samples and on top. Regressions re-tested and all RESOLVED-and-still-fixed: F1 (--delay 1.5 / --at xyz / --delay -5 all rc=1 with clean one-line reasons, broker.log scanned clean of unbound-variable/arithmetic/syntax errors), F7 (--at 99999999999 rejected rc=1, deferred/ stays empty), H1 (topic '../../../tmp/PWNED' rejected rc=1, no /tmp/PWNED created, topics/ empty), F4 (pub/sub --help print usage exit 0), H4 (flood_wedged ran twice clean, ~3s each, no cold-start hang, F3 max-descendants 17 then 19 - flat, no leak). Doc-honesty items confirmed present in README/DEMO and now PREDICT observed behavior: H2 (newline-delimited NUL-free text contract), H3 (at-most-once-modulo-crash), F2 (drops cat uses 2>/dev/null||echo), ceiling (RAM-bound ~2.4MB/sub, explicitly not fd/pid-bound). Only new nit: pstree absent from the container (low, not on my path). No high-severity findings.

claim_audit.claim: fires a --delay 5 message on the exact second while top reads 0% the whole five seconds
claim_audit.evidence: fire-run_at=21ms; top %Cpu 100.0 id and scheduler/socat rows 0.0% across the wait; ps tree sum 0 at every sample

claim_audit.claim: --delay/--at fire AT the deadline (~40ms, wake-driven - not early, not poll-late), with the scheduler idle (0 CPU ticks) during the wait
claim_audit.evidence: measured 21ms lateness; deferred_pub.sh D1/D2 ~35-38ms, D3 0 CPU ticks over 2s

claim_audit.claim: at-most-once-modulo-crash: a deferred message whose deadline elapsed while the broker was down fires immediately on restart into whoever is connected then
claim_audit.evidence: not crash-tested this challenge, but the live decoupling I did observe (late subscriber connecting pre-deadline still received the deferred message) is consistent with broker-side deadlines; doc is honestly scoped

claim_audit.claim: the subscriber ceiling is RAM-bound (~2.4 MB/sub), not fd- or pid-bound
claim_audit.evidence: not stress-tested at scale this challenge; doc now states the honest RAM-bound framing (round-001 'fd/process limits' wording is gone)

proof_assessment.notes: Re-ran tests/chaos_deadline.sh full gate: [1/5] CORRECT missed=0 dup=0 ontime=5000 total_fires=5000 over N=5000 PASS. Negative controls all failed as required: [2/5] naivesleep missed=120/120; [3/5] drainfirst missed=120/120; [4/5] nocommit dup=1770; [5/5] idle CPU 0.00% < 5.0%. The proof spine is intact at version a82ae2a.

dismissal_survived.notes: The naivesleep negative control IS exactly 'blind sleep ignores the wake' and it misses 120/120 - the gate demonstrates the naive approach loses the race while src/sched.sh fires 5000/5000. Dismissal pre-empted by a live must-fail control, not just prose.
